description.en-US: These are the apps that have been archived from the main repo.

summary.en-US: Mobile baseball scorecard, digitizing the paper scoring notation

description.en-US:
This app was co-authored by an AI agent (Claude, by Anthropic), directed
and reviewed by a human. See the project's source repository and git
history for the full record of what was generated versus human-directed.

Backwards K is a mobile-first digital scorecard for keeping score at a
baseball game — built to replace the paper scorecard, not simplify away
from it. It follows the same notation experienced scorers already use,
including the notation the app is named after: a backwards K for a
called third strike.

Features:

* Live scoring with the full paper-scorecard result vocabulary, including
  fielder assist sequences (6-3, 4-6-3, ...)
* Automatic runs/RBI, derived from a baserunning simulation rather than
  manual entry
* Automatic box score, including errors
* Multiple games saved locally, browsable from a Games tab
* Optional MLB Stats API sync to pull a real game's lineups and
  play-by-play into the scorecard
* Non-LLM play-by-play recap generation from a finished game
* Fully offline once installed — no accounts, no tracking
